2008-04-01.title: Turkey Court Takes Politically Explosive Case
2008-04-01.abstract: Turkey’s highest court decided to hear a case seeking to disband the governing party and ban its top leaders from politics.

2008-04-01.title: Zimbabwe Opposition Insists Mugabe Lost
2008-04-01.abstract: The main opposition party pressed its claim that it had won a landslide victory to unseat President Robert G. Mugabe, but there was no official announcement.

2008-04-01.title: Opponents of Musharraf Assume Posts in Pakistan
2008-04-01.abstract: A stern-faced President Pervez Musharraf swore in a new cabinet on Monday that was filled with political opponents from the main opposition parties.

2008-04-01.title: Mexico: American-Owned Bank Bombed
2008-04-01.abstract: A homemade bomb destroyed the entrance of a branch of Citigroup’s Mexican subsidiary, Banamex, in southern Mexico City, causing no injuries. No one claimed responsibility, but small guerrilla groups have previously attacked foreign-owned banks.

2008-04-01.title: Pressed by Rice, Palestinian Leader Agrees to Return to Peace Talks With Israeli
2008-04-01.abstract: The Palestinian president, Mahmoud Abbas, said that he would return to the bargaining table with the Israeli prime minister, Ehud Olmert, next week.

2008-04-02.title: Talks May End Mugabe’s Rule in Zimbabwe
2008-04-02.abstract: Opposition members said that negotiations were under way for President Robert G. Mugabe to step down, while state-run media reported that a runoff is likely.

2008-04-02.title: Corrections: For The Record
2008-04-02.abstract: An article on March 20 about a decision by Iraq’s Presidency Council to withdraw objections to legislation on provincial elections referred incorrectly to the effective date of any Iraqi legislation passed into law. Under Iraq’s constitution, if the effective date is not specified in the legislation, then it is the date the legislation is published in the newspaper of the Ministry of Justice — not 15 days after publication.

2008-04-02.title: Iraqi Deaths Are on the Rise Again During Clashes With Militias
2008-04-02.abstract: The toll of civilian deaths in the Iraqi capital last month reached its highest point since September 2007.

2008-04-02.title: U.S. Alarmed as Some Exports Veer Off Course
2008-04-02.abstract: Officials said the United Arab Emirates were failing to keep U.S. technology from slipping into the wrong hands.

2008-04-02.title: Britain Puts Troop Drawdown on Hold
2008-04-02.abstract: Plans to withdraw about 1,500 soldiers were frozen pending a review of Iraq’s security situation.

2008-04-03.title: Mugabe Foes Win Majority in Zimbabwe
2008-04-03.abstract: President Robert G. Mugabe’s ruling party lost control of the nation’s Parliament. Will the presidency be next?

2008-04-03.title: Investigators in Congo Check Allegations That Peacekeepers Engaged in Torture
2008-04-03.abstract: A Swedish television report said that French soldiers who were participating in a European Union peacekeeping operation in Congo in 2003 tortured a civilian who was being held prisoner.

2008-04-03.title: China Confirms Protests by Uighur Muslims
2008-04-03.abstract: In the northwestern region of Xinjiang, Uighur Muslims protested Chinese rule last month even as Tibetans rioted in the southwest, Chinese officials said.

2008-04-03.title: World Bank Calls on Sovereign Funds to Invest in Africa
2008-04-03.abstract: The president of the World Bank called for the major government-owned sovereign wealth funds of Asia and the Middle East to join with the bank in investing in Africa.

2008-04-03.title: Ireland’s Prime Minister to Resign Amid Corruption Inquiry
2008-04-03.abstract: Denying that he received corrupt payments, Irish Prime Minister Bertie Ahern announced that he would resign next month.

2008-04-04.title: Battle for Hearts & Minds
2008-04-04.abstract: A top terrorism expert believes Abu Yahya Al-Libi has revealed the secret to bringing down the global Jihadist movement.

2008-04-04.title: Corrections: For the Record
2008-04-04.abstract: A headline on a report in the World Briefing column on Tuesday about a posthumous Medal of Honor for Michael A. Monsoor, a member of the Navy Seals who threw himself on a grenade in Iraq to save his colleagues, misstated his rank. He was a petty officer second class; he was not a commissioned “Navy Officer.” Because of an editing error, the report also misstated the number of people who have received the honor since the beginning of the Afghanistan and Iraq wars. Petty Officer Monsoor became the fourth, not the fifth.

2008-04-04.title: Chinese Rights Activist Is Jailed
2008-04-04.abstract: Critics say the conviction of Hu Jia is part of a government crackdown to silence dissidents ahead of the Olympics.

2008-04-04.title: Cargo Ship From Europe Joins Station in Space
2008-04-04.abstract: The Jules Verne cargo ship made a nearly flawless first docking to the International Space Station on Thursday, bringing with it tons of needed supplies.

2008-04-04.title: Former Leader in Kosovo Acquitted of War Crimes
2008-04-04.abstract: Ramush Haradinaj, the former prime minister of Kosovo, was acquitted of all charges of war crimes and crimes against humanity.

2008-04-05.title: Iraqi Premier Suspends Government Raids on Militias
2008-04-05.abstract: Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki said that he was suspending raids “in order to give a chance to those who have repented and want to lay down their weapons.”

2008-04-05.title: Congressional Maneuvers Ahead of Iraq Hearings
2008-04-05.abstract: Democrats and Republicans are laying the groundwork for the coming Congressional testimony of Gen. David H. Petraeus and Ryan C. Crocker, the American ambassador in Iraq.

2008-04-05.title: U.S. Charges Contractor at Iraq Post in Stabbing
2008-04-05.abstract: The charge was made under expanded authority that the United States Congress provided the military to crack down on contractor abuses.

2008-04-05.title: A U.S.-Trained Entrepreneur Becomes Voodoo’s Pope
2008-04-05.abstract: Haiti’s supreme master of voodoo, Max Beauvoir, is a voodoo promoter extraordinaire, with his own Web site and a following among foreigners.

2008-04-05.title: Overcoming Customs and Stigma, Sudan Gives Orphans a Lifeline
2008-04-05.abstract: A shift in local social and religious customs has rescued many infants of Maygoma Orphanage in Khartoum, Sudan, from a grim fate.

2008-04-06.title: On Japan’s Catholic Outposts, Faith Abides Even as the Churches Dwindle
2008-04-06.abstract: On the Goto Archipelago, one quarter of the roughly 25,000 inhabitant are Catholics, by far the highest level in a country where Christianity failed to take root.

2008-04-06.title: Christian Priest Killed at Baghdad Home
2008-04-06.abstract: The priest, who was shot outside his home by attackers who used a silenced pistol, was the second senior Syrian Orthodox priest to be killed this year.

2008-04-06.title: Iraqi Forces and Militia Clash in Baghdad
2008-04-06.abstract: Intense fighting broke out in Sadr City as American and Iraqi troops moved to push out Shiite fighters.

2008-04-06.title: Single Mothers in China Forge a Difficult Path
2008-04-06.abstract: China’s single mothers are often marked as social outcasts in a country that still strictly controls population growth and limits the rights of their children.

2008-04-06.title: Amazon’s ‘Forest Peoples’ Seek a Role in Striking Global Climate Agreements
2008-04-06.abstract: Leaders of hundreds of indigenous groups in 11 Latin American countries sought consensus on a conservation plan for tropical forests like the Amazon.

2008-04-07.title: ‘War Horses’ Differ, but See Hope for Future Accord
2008-04-07.abstract: Meeting for the last time as presidents, Vladimir V. Putin and President Bush again failed to come to agreement on American missile defenses in Europe.

2008-04-07.title: Correction: Olympic Torch Begins Its Journey in Beijing
2008-04-07.abstract: An article on Tuesday about the Olympic torch ceremony in Beijing misstated the name of the street where traffic was blocked for security during the ceremony. It is the Avenue of Eternal Peace, not the Avenue of Heavenly Peace.

2008-04-07.title: Court to Rule on Release of Vote Count in Zimbabwe
2008-04-07.abstract: A court in Harare will decide whether to grant the opposition’s demand for the immediate release of the final tally of last week’s presidential vote, or call for a recount.

2008-04-07.title: U.S. and Iraqis Battle Militias to End Attacks
2008-04-07.abstract: At least three U.S. soldiers were killed and 31 wounded and at least 20 Iraqis were killed in a failed operation to stop militia attacks on Baghdad’s Green Zone.

2008-04-07.title: Kosovo’s Actions Hearten a Hungarian Enclave
2008-04-07.abstract: Romania’s Hungarian minority, known as Szeklers, believe it’s their time for independence and for their proposed semi-autonomous state, Szeklerland.

2008-04-08.title: Negligent Driving Killed Diana, Jury Finds
2008-04-08.abstract: A jury in Britain called the killing of Princess Diana and her lover unlawful, a verdict described as being tantamount to manslaughter.

2008-04-08.title: Attacks in Baghdad Spiked in March, U.S. Data Show
2008-04-08.abstract: The number of attacks against civilians and American and Iraqi security forces in Baghdad more than doubled in March from the previous month.

2008-04-08.title: Middle East Peace Talks Resume
2008-04-08.abstract: Meeting after a break of almost two months, Israelis and Palestinians agreed that peace talks would continue from now on without interruption, hoping to reach an agreement by the end of the year.

2008-04-08.title: Badges Worn by French Athletes Might Violate Olympic Rules
2008-04-08.abstract: The badges with the words “for a better world” may have violated Olympic rules prohibiting political protests.

2008-04-08.title: Kashmir Says Come On In, the Tee Times Are Safe
2008-04-08.abstract: The Indian government is working to rebrand a heavily militarized Himalayan region as a global golfing destination.

2008-04-09.title: Tensions Rising in Sadr City
2008-04-09.abstract: This mostly Shiite district of Baghdad has been encircled by troops since heavy fighting in Basra spilled over into the capital last month.

2008-04-09.title: Tensions Rising in Sadr City
2008-04-09.abstract: This mostly Shiite district of Baghdad has been encircled by troops since heavy fighting in Basra spilled over into the capital last month.

2008-04-09.title: Tensions Rising in Sadr City
2008-04-09.abstract: This mostly Shiite district of Baghdad has been encircled by troops since heavy fighting in Basra spilled over into the capital last month.

2008-04-09.title: Tensions Rising in Sadr City
2008-04-09.abstract: This mostly Shiite district of Baghdad has been encircled by troops since heavy fighting in Basra spilled over into the capital last month.

2008-04-09.title: Corrections: For the Record
2008-04-09.abstract: An article on Monday about the growing number of foreigners in Italy who have excelled as professional chefs of Italian cuisine referred incorrectly to the preparation of guanciale, or pig cheek, an ingredient used in carbonara, a pasta dish. Most guanciale is cured, by salting and drying; it is not smoked.

2008-04-10.title: Iraq’s Military Seen as Lagging
2008-04-10.abstract: Gen. David H. Petraeus’s recommendation to suspend troop reductions reflects an assessment that Iraqi forces remain unprepared to take over the mission of securing their own nation, officials said.

2008-04-10.title: Japan Approves New Bank Chief
2008-04-10.abstract: The vote by Japan’s Parliament ended a long political standoff that had left the bank leaderless at a time of global financial turmoil.

2008-04-10.title: Hamas in Largest Arms Buildup Yet, Israeli Study Says
2008-04-10.abstract: Hamas, the militant group that controls Gaza, is getting help from Syria and Iran as it expands, a study says.

2008-04-10.title: Monks Disrupt Media Tour in China
2008-04-10.abstract: In a public relations setback for China, Buddhist monks waved a Tibetan flag during a government-led tour.

2008-04-10.title: Attack Planner for Al Qaeda Reported Dead
2008-04-10.abstract: Abu Ubaidah al-Masri, who American intelligence officials say was involved in the 2005 bombing of London’s transit system, is believed to have died last December of hepatitis in Pakistan.

2008-04-11.title: Nepalis Line Up to Vote in Election to Revamp Nation
2008-04-11.abstract: The people of Nepal voted on Thursday in landmark elections meant to end a bloody chapter in the country’s history, even as they usher in a whole new set of worries.

2008-04-11.title: South Korea’s President Looks to Repair U.S. Ties
2008-04-11.abstract: In an interview ahead of his first visit to Washington as president next week, President Lee Myung-bak stressed the importance of maintaining U.S. troops on the Korean Peninsula

2008-04-11.title: Zimbabwe Opposition Rejects Runoff
2008-04-11.abstract: The country’s main opposition party said its candidate, Morgan Tsvangirai, would not take part in a runoff because it determined he had won the disputed presidential election.

2008-04-11.title: Olympic Official Calls Protests a ‘Crisis’
2008-04-11.abstract: The president of the Olympic committee called on the authorities in Beijing to improve human rights.

2008-04-11.title: Bush Signals No Further Reduction of Troops in Iraq
2008-04-11.abstract: President Bush said that the senior U.S. commander in Iraq could “have all the time he needs” before reducing troops further.

2008-04-12.title: Pakistan Government Proposes Lifting Media Restrictions
2008-04-12.abstract: Lifting restrictions on the media imposed by President Pervez Musharraf last year was one of the main campaign promises of the former opposition parties that now control Parliament.

2008-04-12.title: Berlusconi, Running Again, Is No Longer Promising ‘Italian Miracle’
2008-04-12.abstract: Gone are many of the trademarks of Silvio Berlusconi in the heat of an election, with his comic-book energy declining to fatigue.

2008-04-12.title: A Bloody Era of Syria’s History Informs a Writer’s Banned Novel
2008-04-12.abstract: When Khaled Khalifa decided to write his latest novel about Syria’s bloody struggle during the ’80s, he knew he was touching a taboo subject.

2008-04-12.title: Germany: Artist’s Body Believed Found
2008-04-12.abstract: The Berlin police said that a body found in the Spree River was in all probability Anna Mikhalchuk, a Russian artist.

2008-04-12.title: Gunmen Kill Aide and In-Law of Iraqi Cleric
2008-04-12.abstract: Iraqi forces imposed a curfew in Najaf, fearing a backlash from Moktada al-Sadr’s Mahdi Army after the assassination by unknown gunmen.

2008-04-13.title: For the West, Many Tough Calls on China
2008-04-13.abstract: The intrusion of politics into the 2008 Olympic Games to be hosted by China has become a major worry for diplomats, athletes and the event’s commercial sponsors.

2008-04-13.title: Southern African Leaders Press Zimbabwe
2008-04-13.abstract: Regional political leaders told the government of President Robert Mugabe that it must permit representatives of the opposition to be present when vote tabulations are verified.

2008-04-13.title: China’s Leader Insists Sovereignty Is at Stake Over Tibet
2008-04-13.abstract: President Hu Jintao of China described the recent Tibetan demonstrations as violent crimes orchestrated by the Dalai Lama.

2008-04-13.title: Deal Reported on Dividing Kenya Cabinet Posts
2008-04-13.abstract: The deal ends a long-running deadlock that had threatened to plunge the country back into violence.

2008-04-13.title: Series of Attacks Hit Afghanistan
2008-04-13.abstract: It was the second deadly attack on road builders in a week.

2008-04-14.title: Rebels at the Border
2008-04-14.abstract: At the border with Sudan in the West Darfur region, Chadian rebels are fighting a proxy war involving two of Africa's most divided nations.

2008-04-14.title: Bush Aide Sees ‘Cop-Out’ in Olympic Boycotts
2008-04-14.abstract: National security adviser Stephen J. Hadley said foreign leaders would be more effective influencing China with the “quiet diplomacy” the United States is pursuing.

2008-04-14.title: A City Where You Can’t Hear Yourself Scream
2008-04-14.abstract: Noise — outrageous, unceasing, pounding noise — is the backdrop to a tense time in Egypt, prompting protests.

2008-04-14.title: Dalai Lama Says His Aides Are Talking to China
2008-04-14.abstract: Yet officials in Beijing continue to portray him as having orchestrated protests in Tibet that have led to a crackdown and violence there.

2008-04-14.title: Zimbabwe Opposition Faces Crucial Court Rulings
2008-04-14.abstract: Zimbabwe’s political opposition is facing crucial court decisions this week that could reshape its electoral stalemate with the governing party of Robert Mugabe.

2008-04-15.title: Tourism Saves a Laotian City but Saps Its Buddhist Spirit
2008-04-15.abstract: The city of Luang Prabang in the mountains of central Laos is being transformed into a replica of itself: its dwellings into guest houses and its rituals into shows for tourists.

2008-04-15.title: 4 Teachers Are Killed in Raid by Islamists on Somali School
2008-04-15.abstract: Witnesses said four teachers, one of them a Briton and two others Kenyans, were killed by Islamist insurgents during a midnight raid on an English-language school.

2008-04-15.title: Devising Survival at Factory in Iraq
2008-04-15.abstract: Despite security fears and an avalanche of cheap imports from China and Syria, Hassan Attiya has managed to keep his shoe business afloat.

2008-04-15.title: Taliban Attack Kills 11 Officers at a Police Post in Afghanistan
2008-04-15.abstract: The attack was yet another illustration of how weak Afghanistan’s police force remains.

2008-04-15.title: Dengue Outbreak Sweeps Through Rio
2008-04-15.abstract: Heavy rains the past month have created a fertile breeding ground for mosquitoes carrying the deadly dengue fever virus, deepening a crisis that has now claimed at least 80 lives.

2008-04-16.title: Canada: Conservative Party Raided
2008-04-16.abstract: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police raided the national headquarters of the governing Conservative Party in Ottawa.

2008-04-16.title: Mexico Drug War Causes Wild West Blood Bath
2008-04-16.abstract: In Ciudad Juárez, a turf war among drug cartels has claimed more than 210 lives this year.

2008-04-16.title: Protest by Zimbabwe’s Opposition Falters as the Army Makes a Show of Force
2008-04-16.abstract: The opposition was protesting a delay by the government in releasing the results of the presidential election.

2008-04-16.title: At Indian Preserves, Tigers Remain King as People Are Coaxed Out
2008-04-16.abstract: Efforts to convince people to leave forests raise the question of the price to pay to save the forests and for whom — humans or animals.

2008-04-16.title: Turkey: 53 Mayors Are Fined for Backing Kurdish TV
2008-04-16.abstract: The mayors were charged with helping rebels write requests to the Danish prime minister not to shut down a Danish-based Kurdish satellite broadcaster.

2008-04-17.title: Russia: Tensions Amid Signs of a Chechen Power Struggle
2008-04-17.abstract: The pro-Kremlin government in Chechnya openly acknowledged the fissures between rival security forces as the southern Russian republic’s Public Chamber condemned the Vostok Battalion.

2008-04-17.title: Corrections
2008-04-17.abstract: A picture caption last Thursday about rioting in Haiti over high food prices misstated President Rene Préval’s position on the issue. He urged Haitians to become agriculturally self-sufficient; he did not say he would urge Haiti’s congress to cut taxes on imported food.

2008-04-17.title: New Russian Leader’s Mandate for Changing Little
2008-04-17.abstract: Dmitri A. Medvedev, who will take office as Russia’s president on May 7, appears likely to defer to his predecessor, Vladimir V. Putin. This seems to be an arrangement that many Russians want.

2008-04-17.title: Times Reporter Is Cleared by Zimbabwe
2008-04-17.abstract: A court in Zimbabwe on Wednesday dismissed charges against a reporter for The New York Times, Barry Bearak, who was arrested for covering the elections.

2008-04-17.title: Iraq Cracks Down on Seat Belt Scofflaws
2008-04-17.abstract: Later this month, traffic police officers all over Iraq will start issuing tickets to anyone who drives without buckling up.

2008-04-18.title: Tensions Rise Over Disputed Zimbabwe Vote
2008-04-18.abstract: The government accused the opposition of plotting to overthrow President Robert Mugabe, and South Africa toughened its position by calling for the release of election results.

2008-04-18.title: Prominent Tibetan Figure Held by China, Friends Say
2008-04-18.abstract: The detention of a high-profile Tibetan broadcaster suggests that the crackdown has not yet run its course.

2008-04-18.title: Study Sees an Advantage for Algae Species in Changing Oceans
2008-04-18.abstract: A microscopic plant that lives in oceans around the world may thrive in the changing ocean conditions of the next century, a team of scientists reported.

2008-04-18.title: Palestinian Official Says Talks With Israelis Yield Little
2008-04-18.abstract: The Palestinian Authority’s foreign minister on Thursday offered an unusually bleak assessment of the negotiations with Israel.

2008-04-18.title: U.S. Lacks a Pakistan Plan, Report Finds
2008-04-18.abstract: The Bush administration has relied too heavily on Pakistan’s military to achieve its counterterrorism goals, according to a searing report by a government auditor.

2008-04-19.title: Putin Denies Reports of His Divorce
2008-04-19.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin bluntly dismissed rumors that he had secretly divorced his wife for the affections of a gymnast less than half of his age.

2008-04-19.title: From a Wheelchair, a Scientist Pries Open South Korean Minds
2008-04-19.abstract: Despite suffering from paralysis two years ago, Dr. Lee Sang-mook remains resolutely focused on his work, and has become a model for disabled people in South Korea.

2008-04-19.title: Oil Bill Protest Shuts Mexican Congress
2008-04-19.abstract: The scene inside the lower house of Congress in Mexico City on Friday morning resembled a college political rally more than a legislative chamber.

2008-04-19.title: South Korea Lifts U.S. Beef Ban
2008-04-19.abstract: The decision removes a key dispute between the two allies, hours before President Bush was to meet South Korea’s new president, Lee Myung-bak, in Washington.

2008-04-19.title: Russian Feud Goes to Court in London
2008-04-19.abstract: A long-running feud between two of the richest post-Soviet entrepreneurs reached the High Court in London on Friday.

2008-04-20.title: College and Money: Ask the Expert
2008-04-20.abstract: April is the month for considering financial-aid offers from colleges and universities. This is not a typical year, however. Good news first: the trend toward no-loan packages at top colleges. Now, the bad: an economy in turmoil and more than 55 lenders withdrawing from the system of federally guaranteed student loans. Will there be trouble securing a loan? How do you compare aid packages? What is this thing called need, anyway?Five financial-aid directors — Sarah Clark Donahue of Harvard, Caesar T. Storlazzi of Yale, Kent McGowan of Buffalo State College (State University of New York), Ronald W. Johnson of the University of California, Los Angeles, and Jorge Rodriguez of St. John’s University, in New York City — address reader questions, both personal and general, until April 22.

2008-04-20.title: Chinese Nationalism on the Internet
2008-04-20.abstract: Nationalistic messages calling for Chinese solidarity and the boycott of foreign goods  have spread like wildfire in Internet chat rooms.

2008-04-20.title: Corrections: For The Record
2008-04-20.abstract: An article last Sunday about a three-month-old program in Afghanistan that enables families of men detained at Bagram Air Base to talk to them using video conference calls attributed an erroneous distinction to the program. While it is the first in Afghanistan, the program is not the first of its kind. (Under a program that began in August, some families in Iraq have been able to talk with men detained far from their homes using video calling.)

2008-04-20.title: U.S. Military Seeks to Widen Pakistan Raids
2008-04-20.abstract: The move has been rebuffed over fears that attacking Pakistani radicals may anger the new government.

2008-04-20.title: Trading Pulpit for Politics, Paraguay Cleric Is Favored
2008-04-20.abstract: A former Roman Catholic bishop who regularly wears sandals and admires Che Guevara has a historic chance to break the ruling party’s 62-year grip on power in Paraguay.

2008-04-21.title: Zimbabweans Flee to South Africa
2008-04-21.abstract: Despite fears of being sent back, many Zimbabweans illegally cross the border into South Africa to escape hunger and violence.

2008-04-21.title: Zimbabweans Flee to South Africa
2008-04-21.abstract: Despite fears of being sent back, many Zimbabweans illegally cross the border into South Africa to escape hunger and violence.

2008-04-21.title: U.S. and Iran Find Common Ground in Iraq’s Shiite Conflict
2008-04-21.abstract: The causes of the convergence between Iran and the United States boil down to the logic of self-interest.

2008-04-21.title: Ex-Cleric Wins Paraguay Presidency, Ending a Party’s 62-Year Rule
2008-04-21.abstract: A former Roman Catholic bishop and self-styled champion of the poor broke a 62-year grip on the presidency by the longest-serving political party in the world.

2008-04-21.title: Cardinal Alfonso López Trujillo, 72, Dies in Rome
2008-04-21.abstract: Cardinal Trujillo was the Vatican’s leading voice in defense of traditional family values and in opposition to abortion, contraception and gay marriage.

2008-04-22.title: Oil Market Rattled by Attack on Tanker
2008-04-22.abstract: A Japanese oil tanker was damaged Monday when it was attacked by a small boat in Middle Eastern waters off the coast of Yemen.

2008-04-22.title: Carter Says Hamas and Syria Are Open to Peace
2008-04-22.abstract: Given the general pessimism surrounding Israeli-Arab peace, the upbeat assessment of former American President Jimmy Carter had a contrarian quality to it.

2008-04-22.title: Russian Court Jails American Pastor
2008-04-22.abstract: The pastor was sentenced to a three year prison term for failing to declare a $25 box of hunting rounds that he had intended to give as a gift to a fellow pastor and hunting enthusiast.

2008-04-22.title: U.S. Identifies Tainted Heparin in 11 Countries
2008-04-22.abstract: Federal officials said they had discovered a clear link between a contaminant in blood thinner from China and 81 deaths in the U.S.

2008-04-22.title: Hussein Henchman Has Heart Attack
2008-04-22.abstract: The U.S. military confirmed Saddam Hussein’s cousin Ali Hassan al-Majid, known as Chemical Ali, was in a hospital in stable condition.

2008-04-23.title: Europe Turns Back to Coal, Raising Climate Fears
2008-04-23.abstract: European countries plan to use coal, generally the dirtiest fuel on earth, in new power plants.

2008-04-23.title: For Haiti’s Jobless, No Cost to Play. But Losers Pay.
2008-04-23.abstract: The beauty of playing dominoes is that it does not require any money to compete, but there are many techniques Haitians use to punish the losers, some of them rather painful.

2008-04-23.title: Next-Door Neighbors Back Bush on Trade
2008-04-23.abstract: Canada and Mexico lent their weight to President Bush’s campaign to expand free trade within the framework of Nafta.

2008-04-23.title: France Sees Its Heritage in Its Crumbling Lighthouses
2008-04-23.abstract: With time, harsh weather and automation, France’s lighthouses are disintegrating. And efforts to save them are an uphill battle.

2008-04-23.title: Maliki Tries to Rally Arabs Behind Iraq
2008-04-23.abstract: Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki voiced frustration with fellow leaders of Arab nations for failing to send ambassadors and relieve Iraq’s debts.

2008-04-24.title: Israel and Syria Hint at Progress on Golan Heights Deal
2008-04-24.abstract: A Syrian cabinet minister said Israel’s premier had indicated that Israel would be willing to withdraw from all the Golan Heights in return for peace with Syria.

2008-04-24.title: Bush and Jordan’s King Meet on Mideast
2008-04-24.abstract: King Abdullah of Jordan told President Bush that negotiations between Palestinians and Israelis “should be based on clear grounds and fixed time frames.”

2008-04-24.title: Maoist Ex-Rebels Are Likely Leaders of Nepal Transition
2008-04-24.abstract: Nepal appeared to be on the verge of Maoist-led rule, as election results signaled that the country’s former guerrillas had won the largest share of seats for a Constituent Assembly.

2008-04-24.title: After Threats, Danes and Dutch Close Embassies Abroad
2008-04-24.abstract: Citing “serious threats,” Denmark closed its embassy in Afghanistan and relocated all staff members, said a spokesman for the Foreign Ministry.

2008-04-24.title: Russian Readers Comment on Article About Religion
2008-04-24.abstract: What do Russians themselves think about the ties between the Russian Orthodox Church and the Kremlin, or about the rights of Protestants?

2008-04-25.title: A Presentation on Syrian/Korean Ties
2008-04-25.abstract: The White House makes its case on a Syrian reactor, with an eye on North Korea, to members of Congress and reporters.

2008-04-25.title: Under Criticism, Bush Talks With Palestinian Leader
2008-04-25.abstract: President Bush is facing mounting criticism from Palestinians over his planned attendance of Israel’s 60th birthday celebration.

2008-04-25.title: Probation, and Remorse, for Figure in Oil-for-Food Case
2008-04-25.abstract: An Iraqi-American businessman who was ensnared in the sprawling investigation into the United Nations’ oil-for-food program was sentenced to three years of probation.

2008-04-25.title: Bush Administration Releases Images to Bolster Its Claims About Syrian Reactor
2008-04-25.abstract: The photographic images were released to support the assertion that the building in Syria that Israel destroyed in an airstrike last year was a nuclear reactor constructed with help from North Korea.

2008-04-25.title: Pakistan Asserts It Is Near a Deal With Militants
2008-04-25.abstract: A 15-point draft of the accord would lead to the gradual withdrawal of the Pakistani military from part of the tribal region of South Waziristan.

2008-04-26.title: Polling Stations Quiet in Iran’s Parliamentary Runoff Election
2008-04-26.abstract: Polls opened in Tehran and other areas of Iran on Friday for runoff elections to fill the 82 seats in the 290-member Parliament in which there was not a clear winner in the first round.

2008-04-26.title: Serbia’s Most Famous Survivor Fears That Recent History Will Repeat Itself
2008-04-26.abstract: Vesna Vulovic, who survived a 33,300-foot plummet from a plane explosion 36 years ago, fears that it is the current political situation in her native Serbia that will kill her.

2008-04-26.title: Zimbabwe Rounds Up Opposition Members and Election Monitors
2008-04-26.abstract: The raid signaled a sharp and very public escalation of the country’s deepening and increasingly violent political crisis.

2008-04-26.title: Mexico: Leftist Lawmakers End Takeover of Congress
2008-04-26.abstract: Leftist politicians said they would end their takeover of Congress, which had shut down legislative business for 16 days as they protested President Felipe Calderón’s plan to overhaul the state’s oil monopoly.

2008-04-26.title: Joint Chiefs Chairman Assails Iran’s Role in Iraq
2008-04-26.abstract: The government of Iran continues to supply weapons and other support to extremists in Iraq, said Adm. Michael G. Mullen.

2008-04-27.title: Conservatives Prevail in Iran Vote, but Opposition Scores, Too
2008-04-27.abstract: The results suggested President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad could face opposition in next year’s presidential campaign.

2008-04-27.title: In Argentina’s Grain Belt, Farmers Revolt Over Taxes
2008-04-27.abstract: Farmers say talks with the national government are going nowhere and are yet more proof that the president does not understand them.

2008-04-27.title: In Zimbabwe Jail: A Reporter’s Ordeal
2008-04-27.abstract: A Times reporter, jailed for “committing journalism” while reporting on the elections in Zimbabwe, writes about his ordeal.

2008-04-27.title: Toronto Transit Workers Strike Suddenly, Stranding Thousands
2008-04-27.abstract: The unexpected strike followed the rejection by 65 percent of unionized transit workers of a tentative contract that had been endorsed by their union’s leadership.

2008-04-27.title: Allegations Lead Army to Review Arms Policy
2008-04-27.abstract: An allegation of fraud prompted the review of procedures used to supply security forces in Afghanistan and Iraq.

2008-04-28.title: Kremlin Joins Russians Keeping an Eye on Prices
2008-04-28.abstract: The Kremlin is keenly sensitive to rising food costs as the country begins to succumb to a global trend that has sparked riots in poorer countries.

2008-04-28.title: Karzai Escapes Attack in Kabul by Gunmen
2008-04-28.abstract: The attack by suspected Taliban insurgents on a military parade in the capital, which killed three, was embarrassing for the Afghan government.

2008-04-28.title: Signs of Attacks on Zimbabwe’s Opposition
2008-04-28.abstract: There is growing evidence of widespread retribution against people who supported the opposition party.

2008-04-28.title: Iraq Struggle Unfolds in Peaceful Protest and Violent Attacks in Sadr City
2008-04-28.abstract: A demonstration against violence was held by members of the Iraqi Parliament even as clashes went on nearby.

2008-04-28.title: Legislators End Toronto Transit Strike
2008-04-28.abstract: A bill ordering 8,900 striking transit workers to return to work provides fines of 2,000 Canadian dollars a day those who refuse to do so.

2008-04-29.title: North Korean Officer Defects to the South
2008-04-29.abstract: The soldier was the first Communist officer to defect in a decade, a South Korean military spokesman said.

2008-04-29.title: Austria Stunned by Case of Imprisoned Woman
2008-04-29.abstract: Austrian police have arrested a 73-year-old man who they say kept his daughter locked in a cellar for 24 years and fathered seven children with her.

2008-04-29.title: Gazan Mother and 4 Children Killed
2008-04-29.abstract: A Palestinian mother and her four young children were killed in Gaza during an Israeli operation against militants, and a dispute quickly arose over exactly how they had died.

2008-04-29.title: Indian Prime Minister Denounces Abortion of Females
2008-04-29.abstract: Prime Minister Manmohan Singh described the growing practice of aborting female fetuses as a “national shame.”

2008-04-29.title: Zimbabwe Opposition Reunites
2008-04-29.abstract: The two opposition groups, formally reunited, claimed control of the Parliament for the first time in the nation’s history and demanded that President Robert Mugabe concede.

2008-04-30.title: Chechnya’s Capital Rises From the Ashes, Atop Hidden Horrors
2008-04-30.abstract: There are 57 known but unopened mass graves in the republic of Chechnya, where construction has taken over in what used to be a war zone.

2008-04-30.title: DNA Tests Proves Austrian Fathered Daughter’s Children
2008-04-30.abstract: Investigators continued their inquiries into Josef Fritzl, but the head of the criminal investigations unit in Lower Austria, Franz Polzer, called the DNA tests “decisive evidence.”

2008-04-30.title: China Sends 30 to Prison in Tibet Riots
2008-04-30.abstract: The sentences are the first punishments meted out to those accused of taking part in last month’s riots.

2008-04-30.title: For 100 Iraqi Doctors, a Return to Normal
2008-04-30.abstract: A cardiology conference in Baghdad, the first since 2003, was a triumph, even as two-thirds of the medical specialists has fled the country and the health care systems is in shreds.

2008-04-30.title: Serbia: The European Union Cracks the Door in Advance of Vote
2008-04-30.abstract: The European Union signed a pre-membership pact with Serbia as a way to help pro-Western forces avoid defeat by nationalists at the polls on May 11.
